ip_outcome_note: Confirmed via PokemonTCG.io swsh6-47 name field. National Pokedex no. 179. Johto Electric-type sheep. Reverse Holofoil variant.

trivia_bullets.text: Mareep's name is likely a combination of 'Mary' (from 'Mary Had a Little Lamb') and 'sheep.' The name is also an anagram of 'ampere,' foreshadowing the evolution line's endpoint Ampharos. The Japanese name Merriep combines 'merry,' the sheep vocalization 'mē,' and 'sheep.'

Mareep and its evolution line are widely considered a reference to Philip K. Dick's 1968 novel 'Do Androids Dream of Electric Sheep?' (the source novel for Blade Runner). The electric sheep premise maps directly onto an Electric-type sheep Pokemon.
sources: GameFAQs: 'Do Androids Dream of Mareep?' thread
Game Informer: Sableye and Mareep design origins, 2015

trivia_bullets.text: "Rubbing its fleece generates electricity. You'll want to pet it because it's cute, but if you use your bare hand, you'll get a painful shock." The static-friction electricity mechanic mirrors how real wool builds up triboelectric charge.

Corpus note: this is a Reverse Holofoil print. The archive now holds at least three Mareep/Flaaffy prints across sets, making the electric-sheep evo pair one of the more represented Johto lines in the collection.
